response.justification: Strong technical leader with 15+ years of engineering experience across multiple domains and recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure, but lacks the demonstrated AI expertise and advanced degree in AI that the principal-level AI strategy role emphasizes. Current AI work at re:cinq (4 months) is too recent to establish 'recognized expertise,' and no prior track record of shipping AI products or platforms.
strengths: Exceptional technical breadth and depth—MIT CS degree with 15+ years spanning web, mobile, embedded, infrastructure, and now AI agents; proven ability to master complex domains quickly

Strong engineering leadership trajectory—Staff Engineer role, multiple senior positions, founder of GISCollective, organized technical workshops; clear ability to inspire and guide teams on difficult problems
Current AI-adjacent expertise—actively contributing to MCP code generators and agentic frameworks at re:cinq, demonstrating hands-on builder fluency with emerging AI infrastructure
concerns: AI experience is nascent—only 4 months into AI-focused work; lacks demonstrated shipping of AI products or platforms required for 'recognized expertise' at principal level
Missing preferred advanced credentials—BS in CS but no Master's or PhD in AI/ML; educational foundation does not signal deep AI specialization
No prior AI strategy track record—15 years of career shows no history of identifying, designing, or scaling AI-driven solutions; entrepreneurial experience (GISCollective) is geo-data, not AI-focused

key_factors.factor: AI expertise gap vs. role emphasis
Recent AI infrastructure exposure but no proven shipping record
